editorialNotes: Production replacement rollup preserves the May 6 dev prerelease themes after v26.5.629 exposed mobile Friends graph selection click noise before publishing assets.

release.deck: Freed Desktop gets a broad stability pass across startup repair, WebKit memory pressure, dense Friends and Map rendering, Google Drive sync, and mobile PWA reading.
features: Mobile PWA polish balances the toolbar, tightens the menu and settings surfaces, moves support into a dedicated modal, and fixes reader spacing and fixed action menus.
Friends and Map views scale better with dense data through lighter graph avatars, stable marker data while moving, faster map count preparation, and new performance budgets.
Google Drive sync is more resilient with safer cloud clients, upload retries, and better progress handling for larger local documents.
fixes: Startup repair now covers AppKit activation, regular app activation, native show retries, unminimized windows, front ordering, scrollable recovery dialogs, and clearer activation diagnostics.
Renderer recovery is calmer under memory pressure, hidden heartbeats, stale windows, background work, and WebKit timer throttling.
Memory pressure gates now use macOS physical footprint, keep watching WebKit RSS during locked and hidden sessions, and trim oversized WebKit caches before social scrapes.
Social scraping now backs off under pressure while keeping Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X, and inline replies more reliable.
Feed and reader polish improves card gutters, visible archive batching, saved signal filters, scroll read tracking, compact reader rails, and short article spacing.
Google Contacts, snapshots, RSS startup polling, release channel state, and update checks keep their state more consistently across startup and recovery paths.
Dense Map markers preserve recovered location labels and suppress decorative chrome during movement so panning stays responsive.
Friends graph selection, Details mode, pinch zoom, relationship sliders, channel linking, and graph persistence are covered by steadier desktop and mobile tests.

Release validation coverage now matches the restored mobile settings overview, selected Friends Details behavior, browser-level graph pinch precision, stable Map and Friends action paths, remount-safe Friends graph geometry reads, and mobile graph selection without CI-sensitive synthetic canvas clicks.

Continue dense surface sweeps for any remaining frame budget misses.

releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed v26.5.630

Freed Desktop gets a broad stability pass across startup repair, WebKit memory pressure, dense Friends and Map rendering, Google Drive sync, and mobile PWA reading.

### Features

- Mobile PWA polish balances the toolbar, tightens the menu and settings surfaces, moves support into a dedicated modal, and fixes reader spacing and fixed action menus.
- Friends and Map views scale better with dense data through lighter graph avatars, stable marker data while moving, faster map count preparation, and new performance budgets.
- Google Drive sync is more resilient with safer cloud clients, upload retries, and better progress handling for larger local documents.

### Fixes

- Startup repair now covers AppKit activation, regular app activation, native show retries, unminimized windows, front ordering, scrollable recovery dialogs, and clearer activation diagnostics.
- Renderer recovery is calmer under memory pressure, hidden heartbeats, stale windows, background work, and WebKit timer throttling.
- Memory pressure gates now use macOS physical footprint, keep watching WebKit RSS during locked and hidden sessions, and trim oversized WebKit caches before social scrapes.
- Social scraping now backs off under pressure while keeping Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X, and inline replies more reliable.
- Feed and reader polish improves card gutters, visible archive batching, saved signal filters, scroll read tracking, compact reader rails, and short article spacing.
- Google Contacts, snapshots, RSS startup polling, release channel state, and update checks keep their state more consistently across startup and recovery paths.
- Dense Map markers preserve recovered location labels and suppress decorative chrome during movement so panning stays responsive.
- Friends graph selection, Details mode, pinch zoom, relationship sliders, channel linking, and graph persistence are covered by steadier desktop and mobile tests.
- Release validation coverage now matches the restored mobile settings overview, selected Friends Details behavior, browser-level graph pinch precision, stable Map and Friends action paths, remount-safe Friends graph geometry reads, and mobile graph selection without CI-sensitive synthetic canvas clicks.

### Follow-ups

- Keep monitoring installed WebKit RSS after renderer projection slimming and cache trimming.
- Continue dense surface sweeps for any remaining frame budget misses.
- Use measured performance budget misses to choose the next optimization target.
- Watch the new release validation coverage for real regressions instead of pixel-level synthetic gesture noise, remount timing races, locator geometry churn, and canvas hit-target noise.

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
